reviews.review_description:
1.	You should make the wars a little more interactive, because I like the way WorldBox does it where a bunch of people go in and fight instead of just a small squadron being able to take over a city on the first try.
	2.	I would like more races and more kingdoms being able to make kingdoms.
	3.	It would be cool if you could become a person in the world and actually interact with the world and play the game as a human being.
	4.	I would like to be able to favorite people so I can remember certain people that I want to find again.

I love the game right now but it could so much better like more realism but not in graphics maybe you could control someone or make another game mode where you are stuck as a person and you build the world like minecraft also make it so you can make the map bigger and more biomes just don’t give up on the game and add ideologies like republics, communists, and socialist kingdoms/ country’s or maybe empires also add more planets to conquer, adding on that can you allow empires or kingdoms to move between planets so you can expand your empire. Add other empires on other planets that are also trying to expand their empire. Make it so you can change peoples names.

Definitely not perfect, but still good. Wish There were more good/Evil Aesthetic and look based thing or even gameplay mechanics, Would compare to some of the other ones I've seen. This isn't too bad. Closest thing to Black and White, you'll find.

It’s a great game 10/10 but one thing lacks and it’s missing multiplayer could you imagine you and your friends playing it making your own world! has good graphics and it’s a very good game that gets you hooked but it’s just missing multiplayer, love your game developers keep up the good work￼

reviews.review_description: 10/10 game. Really loved it, but as other reviews mentioned this piece of art can be a game changer for God Simulators if devs won’t give up and continue adding content and mechanics. Always checking the Meta VR app to see if there’s any update available for Deisim.

Any one who is reading this get the game no questions asked best buy of my life as of the devs you have really worked on your game and its one of my favorites, thank you for all your hard work and effort. It’s seen and it’s appreciated. And I’d love to see more in the future!! Like their character traits or just mark them so you can keep track of them and find them easier. Little things like that would be truly amazing. But you have a really polished game and I love it.
I've dumped so many hours into this beautiful game. It's so fun and fast pace. I like to sit down and watch my civilizations thrive and listen to snooks podcast or just music. It's very calming

This game has consistently been the only game that I keep going back too for more, when I first got into VR this game was a constant for me I was always playing it, and that was years ago when the game was still very bare bones. I don’t use my VR much at all anymore, maybe once or twice every 6 months or so, and I’ve missed allot of updates, and still this game is even more entertaining than before. Genuinely the only vr game that I can stand and play for hours and hours without getting bored, from what I’ve seen from other people reviews and the things in the updates it seems like the developer(s) really listen to what the player base wants and you can only ever respect that, this game genuinely just gets better everytime I play it.

Can’t wait to come back to this game in another 6 months and see how much better it’s gotten again, good job dude(s) you’ve made an amazing game:)
It's a very neat concept where you essentially play as God. A lot more fun that I thought lol

developer_response.body: When one of your city reach the futuristic age and have access to titanium they will start to send space shuttles to build a big space cruiser in the sky. reviews.review_description: I love how, in this game, you can create your own world, take care of your little guys, and build kingdoms. If anyone is trying to find a fun sandbox game, this is the one y’all are looking for — it’s amazing.

reviews.review_title: The BEST sandbox godsim on quest
reviews.review_description:
You can jump into this game without knowing anything and understand it very quickly. The graphics are easy on the eye, building worlds is so fun and there’s a LOT to uncover. Your cities and kingdoms start out as nothing and advance through countless stages all the way to futuristic, theres many biomes and each one has different types of civilizations to discover, same with animals and mines. 

You have many options at your disposal so you can create natural disasters from thin air and effect the way kingdoms are perceived, you can also heal people, give them aspects like immortality, and your kingdoms go to war with each other. The environment is fully interactable so you can grab, move, kill, or destroy literally anything. 

Civilizations interact with each other via trade so having seas that connect them is important, cuz they use boats to transport their goods. Certain biomes have materials that others don’t, so people will want to trade. The game will get you hooked and is literally the perfect thing for a creative person, like you can dump dozens to hundreds of hours in this game. Ive played for a while and have yet to find any bugs.

On another note, the developers are updating the game way more so now is the time to get it (we have dinosaurs and fantasy now). if you are reading this, wondering if you should buy Deisim, I promise you won’t regret it.
Like a mode where you can be a civ where you can do civ jobs and the miracle menu is replaced with civ tools and new maps like mars and the moon

This game is an absolute blast, and is a real breath of fresh air from the physics based or gorilla tag based games

I absolutely love Deisem — it’s hands-down one of the most immersive VR god simulators ever, and I can’t get enough of shaping civilizations, creating worlds, and playing as a true deity. The addition of characters from the developer’s other game, Underworld Overseer, was a brilliant touch, and I’d love to see them get their own special island. I also dream of a Dinosaur Island, where massive prehistoric creatures roam freely and civilizations must survive among them — it would make exploration wild, dangerous, and endlessly fun.

The game could reach even greater heights with new people, civilizations, and unique cultures, each with their own traits, skills, and quirks. A story mode, or a controllable story mode where you guide events as they unfold, could give the game a fresh narrative layer while still letting players enjoy the sandbox freedom. Imagine being able to influence politics, trade, cultural festivals, or religious events while shaping terrain, controlling weather, triggering earthquakes, or sending plagues — the god-like possibilities are endless.

Other exciting ideas include: mythical creatures, rare beasts, magical resources, and secret hidden civilizations, as well as random world events, like cosmic phenomena, natural disasters, or mysterious omens, that make every playthrough unique. Expanding biomes — enchanted forests, floating islands, volcanic lands, ice mountains, underwater realms, or even sky kingdoms — would make exploration feel limitless. Players could interact with the world in VR through motion gestures, lifting mountains, redirecting rivers, or summoning storms, making every action feel physically powerful and immersive. Multiplayer would allow friends to co-create, compete, or collaborate, adding a whole new layer of fun.

Replayability would be incredible with multiple endings, evolving civilizations that remember your actions, hidden islands, unlockable miracles, seasonal events, and achievements for creative gameplay. Whether you prefer a peaceful guiding hand, a chaotic trickster god, or an all-powerful wrathful deity, Deisem gives players the freedom to play their own way in a truly living, breathing world.

Overall, Deisem is already a standout VR experience, but with ideas like these — Dinosaur Island, Underworld Overseer island, story mode, new civilizations, mythical creatures, secret events, multiplayer, and deep VR interactions — it could become one of the most ambitious, expansive, and unforgettable god simulators ever made. I genuinely can’t wait to see how this game evolves because it’s already a masterpiece.
Listen to brewsobro’s rating with game ideas

reviews.review_title: Being a god is incredibly boring
reviews.review_description:
The "god game" concept is intriguing at first, but in reality, it feels very hollow. Once you've guided your civilization to the end for the first time, there’s absolutely nothing left to do.
Even the updates and the "Underworld" DLC don't save the experience — they don't add real depth, just more of the same repetitive tasks. The gameplay becomes a chore: you just place tiles and watch the same cycles over and over. There’s no soul or engaging story to bring you back. After one full playthrough, it became so boring that I don't even want to open the app anymore. I guess being a god is just a very boring job, at least in this game.
If u like worldbox, u will like this

reviews.review_title: Super buggy and terrible movement scheme
reviews.review_description:
I installed this game and am able to make it about 3 minutes in before it bugs out and refuses to progress. I get to a point where it says the civilization needs 100 meat, but no animals spawn naturally anywhere and I don't see any way or power to make them spawn. I've watched several guides and restarted numerous times but to no avail.

Also, the movement scheme is atrocious. If you want to move up or down, you have to look up and go forward or look down and move backwards. Both joysticks do the same thing, but one should do camera rotations and one should do movement through the world. Also, the smooth turning needs to be faster. I can literally feel myself aging in the time it takes to turn around.

Bit of UX advice for the devs: the menu shouldn't show up obscured behind game objects, it should render on top of everything else in the game to guarantee you can always interact with it.

developer_response.body:
Sorry you ran into issues early on, that’s not a good first experience.

For the 100 meat part, it’s possible you didn’t have the Animal Miracle unlocked yet, since it only becomes available once your world is a bit bigger. Once unlocked, that’s how you place animals manually. That’s on me for not making that clearer, I’ll improve it.

For movement, I’d recommend using the grip controls, they’re usually a lot more comfortable and precise than joystick movement.

I appreciate the feedback on turning speed and the menu as well, I’ll take a look at both.

If you want, feel free to join the Discord (discord.deisim.com), I’m pretty active there and can help directly if you run into anything else.

reviews.review_title: A great game that is much too short
reviews.review_description: It’s well made, and the ideas and implementation of those ideas are well done, but you will finish everything interesting fast. And once you know how to complete it, it’ll only be faster on the next runs. I just wish once the “story” is over, there were more random events or things that happen that add randomness, to make each run feel much different. The first run is insanely fun. But once you beat it, you’ll just see some occasional armies gathering to capture another territory (which in my experience), just has the attacking army winning every single time. There’s maybe 2-3 natural disasters that you can prevent or allow to attack your world. There’s not much else from there, the end game is very dull. I wish they’d add some way to make the endgame feel very random. There’s just not enough for the normal price tag chosen for this game. It’s predictable and after 2-3 runs, unentertaining. I’d get this game on sale if you’re considering it. Might even wait a long time for more updates if I was new buyer.

reviews.review_title: Hmm feels lacking but it’s fun…
reviews.review_description:
This game has potential, I loved it for the first 25 mins or so and then I got a little bored.
Having a game that’s even half as good as black and white for PC back in the day would be so freaking cool.

This game has incredible potential to be with us for the next 10+ years. To me, it’s a bit like an ant farm type game. Throw some stuff down and see how the humans make it through. 

It’s a lot of fun to build an entire world to your liking and then put 2 humans down then just watch. 

As of right now, there doesn’t seem to be a point in the “heretics” and “faith” in general. I would assume villages with lower faith would do wacky stuff like sacrifice their people for “favors” and the more aggressive they are, the more they’d try to spread their anti faith. Then of course on the flip side, villages with higher faith will be more loyal and attempt to spread faith through missionaries.

As a deity, in sandbox, we should have the ability to “favor” a certain kingdom or people. We should be able to drop raw materials for them to use such as wood, stone, food, etc.

Lastly, pirates are a fun little thing to mix stuff up, but as civilizations progress through the ages, they should have some naval ships to protect the traders. Same for airships.

Super awesome potential and looking forward to the updates!

developer_response.body: We just released an update today with military ships and helicopters to hunt Pirates. I hope you will enjoy it :)

reviews.review_description: Love all aspects of this game. But, I did lower my review as there have not been any updates to the game to make it any better.

developer_response.body: We are releasing monthly updates and the last one was released 2 weeks ago.

Also, when you increase the game speed to 2x, it only lasts for a couple minutes and then returns to normal… I wish that would stay on until I want it off. Other than that, I love the game. It’s entertaining and relaxing, time easily slips away and I become quite immersed into the world.

Came back after not playing for a while and was happy they’re finally updating it after 2 years, so I’m happy about that, but I wish the animals had their own way of reproducing instead of having to respawn a new animal or use the regenerate miracle, also wish there was more to do with space travel. Also kind of unrelated but unless they’re royalty their job just says “worker”, maybe give each of them actual jobs based on their traits and health and make them only do certain things related to that job.
